<a id="entry-adopt-project-narrative"></a>

## Entry 1 — 2026-07-23 — Adopt Project Narrative

*Kind: governance. Status: accepted.*

## Context

OntologyServerBuilder teaches junior developers how to build and govern a production-quality
ontology service. The guide makes durable choices about architecture, security boundaries,
development sequence, and teaching method, but a Git commit alone does not preserve why those
choices were made. The repository also teaches learners to adopt Project Narrative in the service
they build, creating a reasonable expectation that the guide applies the same discipline to itself.

The repository should remain approachable. Recording every wording change or prompt refinement
would turn the decision history into a second changelog and add process without useful memory.

## Decision

Adopt `jamiemitchellconsultants/Narrative` as OntologyServerBuilder's decision-history mechanism.
Pull requests that make meaningful product, architecture, governance, operational, correction, or
experimental decisions will carry `narrative-required` and provide exact Narrative Context,
Narrative Decision, and Narrative Consequences sections.

After a qualifying pull request merges, automation will open a separate draft pull request
containing a proposed fragment and regenerated `Narrative.md`. Human review of that proposal remains
the authority that accepts the wording. Mechanical changes—including typo fixes, link repairs,
formatting, and prompt wording that does not change teaching intent—will remain outside Narrative.

## Consequences

The repository practices the governance model it teaches and retains the rationale behind changes
to its learning philosophy and technical contract. Contributors must classify meaningful changes
and provide explicit decision evidence, while routine maintenance stays lightweight.

The repository gains configuration, validation, and post-merge automation. Maintainers must keep
the integration aligned with the upstream Narrative contract and review generated narrative
proposals separately. `Narrative.md` is generated from reviewed fragments and must never be edited
directly.
